steps.output: Contact logged in CRM; prospect record created; channel and source code recorded
Needs, financial situation, existing coverage and risk profile assessed via Needs Analysis Framework
Suitable product identified or client referred; suitability decision documented in CRM

swim_phases.name: Needs analysis & suitability
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–4 · contact to PDS
swim_phases.note: Needs analysis and a documented suitability decision precede PDS delivery; suitability doubts escalate to Member Care.

swim_phases.name: Application completion
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–8 · collect to confirm
swim_phases.note: Applications are captured with material information verbatim and either auto-accepted or referred before terms are confirmed by read-back.

swim_phases.name: Issuance & post-sale
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 9–12 · pay to record
swim_phases.note: On payment the policy issues; a sample of interactions is quality-reviewed and the sale is recorded with a review date.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-010 against the Guidewire PolicyCenter conduct-aligned sales catalog
detail_slides: Steps 1–5 vs Guidewire PolicyCenter conduct catalog
Steps 6–10 vs Guidewire PolicyCenter conduct catalog
Steps 11–13 vs Guidewire PolicyCenter conduct catalog

items.name: Needs analysis framework
items.detail: A structured Needs Analysis Framework with documented assessment aligns with PolicyCenter suitability intake.

items.name: Documented suitability decision
items.detail: Suitability decisions documented in CRM with referral where unsuitable align with conduct controls.

items.name: Agent ready-to-sell enforcement
items.detail: Licensing is a stated prerequisite but not enforced. The catalog blocks selling by representatives not currently licensed and appointed for the product (links to SOP-INS-005).

items.name: PDS delivery & e-consent
items.detail: PDS is provided with verbal/portal acknowledgement, but the catalog mandates an auditable digital consent with a captured timestamp — verbal receipt is hard to evidence.

items.name: Vulnerable-customer detection
items.detail: A vulnerable-customer protocol exists but relies on representative judgement; the catalog adds systematic vulnerability indicators and handling.

items.name: Material information capture
items.detail: Verbatim capture of material information in CRM aligns with PolicyCenter disclosure-of-material-facts control.

items.name: Application submission & auto-accept
items.detail: Rules-based auto-accept/referral present, but no predictive scoring to widen straight-through binding.

items.name: KYC / sanctions screening
items.detail: No identity/sanctions screening before binding. PolicyCenter screens applicants as a regulatory control.

items.name: Referral handling & SLA
items.detail: Referral with client advisory and SLA communication aligns with PolicyCenter referral workflow.

items.name: Read-back confirmation
items.detail: Repeating terms, premium and start date back to the client aligns with PolicyCenter conduct confirmation.

items.name: Payment, issuance & dispatch
items.detail: Payment with mandate setup and issuance per dispatch timelines align with PolicyCenter billing/issuance.

items.name: Post-sale conduct QA
items.detail: Only 10% of interactions are manually reviewed. The catalog QA's conduct at scale with call/interaction analytics, not a small manual sample.

items.name: Sale record & review date
items.detail: CRM sale record with an annual review date aligns with PolicyCenter post-sale management.

critical_missing.title: Agent ready-to-sell enforcement
critical_missing.detail: Licensing is a stated prerequisite but not enforced at point of sale. The catalog blocks selling by representatives not currently licensed and appointed (links to SOP-INS-005).

critical_missing.title: KYC / sanctions screening
critical_missing.detail: No identity/sanctions screening before binding, a regulatory control required by the catalog.

critical_missing.title: Auditable PDS e-consent & suitability e-signature
critical_missing.detail: PDS receipt is verbal/portal and suitability is documented free-text; the catalog mandates an auditable digital consent and signed suitability record — a conduct and evidencing gap.

critical_missing.title: Conduct QA at scale & vulnerability detection
critical_missing.detail: Only 10% of interactions are manually reviewed and vulnerability relies on judgement; the catalog QA's conduct at scale with analytics and systematic vulnerability indicators.

control_bars.name: Ready-to-sell
control_bars.note: Licensing not enforced at point of sale

control_bars.name: Post-sale management
control_bars.note: Sale record with annual review date

remediations.title: Enforce agent ready-to-sell at point of sale
remediations.detail: Block selling by representatives not currently licensed/appointed for the product, integrated with the licensing system (SOP-INS-005). Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Add KYC / sanctions screening
remediations.detail: Screen identity and sanctions before binding; route hits to Compliance. Effort: 4 weeks.

remediations.title: Capture auditable PDS e-consent & suitability e-signature
remediations.detail: Replace verbal PDS receipt with auditable digital consent and a signed suitability record. Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Move conduct QA to analytics at scale
remediations.detail: QA conduct across all interactions with call/interaction analytics, replacing 10% sampling, and add systematic vulnerability indicators. Effort: 5 weeks.

remediations.title: Add suitability decision support & predictive scoring
remediations.detail: Provide next-best-product decision support and a predictive risk score to widen straight-through binding. Effort: 5 weeks.

opt_steps.output: Logged in CRM; prospect record; source code
Selling enabled only if licensed and appointed for the product
Needs, finances, coverage and risk via Needs Analysis Framework
Next-best-product decision support; suitability decision documented
Vulnerability indicators applied; handling per protocol
Digital consent captured with timestamp before application
Material information confirmed and recorded verbatim
Identity and sanctions screened before binding

opt_steps.activity: Submit with predictive scoring; refer if needed
opt_steps.output: Rules plus predictive score widen straight-through binding; refer otherwise
Final premium, options, start date repeated back; confirmation obtained
Payment processed; mandate set up if instalment
Policy number; documents per SOP-INS-004 timelines
All interactions QA'd with call/interaction analytics; coaching automated
Policy, product, premium and payment recorded; annual review date set

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Guidewire PolicyCenter Conduct-Aligned Sales Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v2.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-010. Alignment to the Guidewire PolicyCenter conduct-aligned sales catalog increased from 70% to 91%. Critical additions: agent ready-to-sell enforcement, KYC/sanctions screening, auditable PDS e-consent with a signed suitability record, conduct QA at scale via analytics, and systematic vulnerability detection.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the PolicyCenter catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional controls or automation

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines the channel-agnostic process for signing up a new client across all product lines through the agent or call centre channel. Version 2.0 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Guidewire PolicyCenter Conduct-Aligned Sales Best-Practice Catalog, achieving 91% alignment (up from 70% in v1.3).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to all new policy sales by a licensed agent or call centre representative across personal and commercial product lines where a product-specific SOP does not supersede it, for telephone and face-to-face interactions.
Scope extended: ready-to-sell enforcement, KYC/sanctions screening and PDS e-consent now apply to every sale before binding.
This procedure does NOT cover digital direct sales (SOP-INS-001), group enrolments (SOP-INS-006), or renewals (SOP-INS-004).

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Runs needs/suitability; discloses terms with e-consent; completes application; confirms understanding.
Second-level support; reviews suitability concerns; owns vulnerable-customer handling.
Processes applications in PAS; issues documents; sets up premium with Finance.
Approves exceptions; owns analytics-driven post-sale quality.

sections.title: 5. Conduct & Screening Controls (New Section — v2.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the PolicyCenter catalog. Ready-to-sell, KYC/sanctions and PDS e-consent now gate every sale.
Identity and watchlist screened before bind

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Agent records the declination and stated reason in CRM.
If the client proceeds against recommendation, a suitability override form is completed and e-signed by the client.
All overrides are reviewed by a Team Leader within 24 hours.
If suitability is uncertain, the interaction is paused and escalated to Member Care or a Team Leader.
No product is sold where a material suitability doubt exists without second-level approval.
7.3 Vulnerable or Financially Distressed Client (Enhanced — v2.0)
Systematic vulnerability indicators now support representative judgement in identifying at-risk clients.
Vulnerable-customer protocols apply; the representative does not proceed if the product may not be in the client's best interest.
The client is referred to financial counselling or government assistance where appropriate.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: Contact, needs analysis, suitability and post-sale records
Quotation, application and payment for the agent channel
Conduct QA at scale and coaching
Premium collection at point of sale

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v2.0: ready-to-sell enforcement, KYC/sanctions, PDS e-consent, analytics-based conduct QA and vulnerability detection.
Ready-to-sell gate: no selling by an unlicensed or unappointed representative.
KYC/sanctions: identity and watchlists screened before binding.
Evidenced consent: PDS e-consent and signed suitability record captured before application.
Vulnerability: systematic indicators support fair treatment of at-risk clients.
Conduct QA: all interactions reviewed via analytics, replacing 10% sampling.
Suitability: documented decision with override governance.
Immutable audit trail: needs, consent, screening, sale and QA events logged.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Updated PDS delivery requirements; aligned KPIs to Salesforce reporting
OPTIMISED: ready-to-sell enforcement; KYC/sanctions screening; PDS e-consent & suitability e-signature; analytics-based conduct QA; vulnerability detection; catalog alignment 70% → 91%
